title: Establish Credibility
intro:
STEP 2 OF 8 · MODULE 2

Establish Credibility

Mission: Create authority before money enters the conversation. The customer must conclude, on their own, “these people know what they’re doing.”

OBJECTIVE
BUILD AUTHORITY
TIME WINDOW
90-SEC TOUR
PRIMARY KPI
TOUR → DVI CONSENT
FAILURE MODE
BRAGGING

sections.title: The Psychology — WHY Credibility Works
sections.html:
The Psychology — WHY Credibility Works

Credibility is not what we say about ourselves. Credibility is what the customer concludes after seeing the evidence. A confident claim (“we’re the best”) triggers skepticism. A demonstrated detail (“here’s our certified tech, here’s the loaner fleet, here’s the warranty”) triggers conclusion. Show, don’t tell. The shop tour exists precisely because seeing the operation is 100× more persuasive than describing it.

Credibility lives in three legs — Competence, Transparency, Trustworthiness. We do not pick one. We score all three, every time. (See Figure 0.3, the Trust Triangle.)

LEG 1

Competence

What it answers: “Do they know what they’re doing?”
Evidence: ASE-certified techs, dealer-alternative positioning, equipment, training credentials, years in business.

LEG 2

Transparency

What it answers: “Are they hiding anything?”
Evidence: Shop tour, digital vehicle inspections with photos/video, written estimates before any work, “Meet-or-Beat” policy.

LEG 3

Trustworthiness

What it answers: “Would they do this for their own family?”
Evidence: Warranty (3yr/36k nationwide), loaners/shuttle/Uber, reviews, willingness to defer non-safety work, “whether with us or elsewhere.”

sections.title: The Shop Tour as Authority Play
sections.html:
The Shop Tour as Authority Play

The tour is a 90-second condensed proof package. Each line item in the tour script is engineered to score one or more legs of the trust triangle. Coaches should be able to point at any sentence in the tour and say “that scores Competence” or “that scores Trustworthiness.” If a line scores nothing, cut it.

Tour LineWhat It’s Really DoingTrust Leg Scored
“your certified dealer alternative”Authority-by-association — puts you in dealer category at independent prices.Competence
“ASE and dealer-certified technicians”External validation. Customer doesn’t have to take your word.Competence
“3-year / 36,000-mile nationwide warranty”Risk reversal. Signals long-term confidence.Trustworthy
“shuttle, Uber, loaner, rental reimbursement”Removes the “I can’t leave my car” objection. Reciprocity trigger.Trustworthy
“digital inspections, electronic approvals”Transparency made visible. Nothing happens in secret.Transparency
“Can I get you coffee or water?”Reciprocity. Tiny gift creates a tiny obligation.Trustworthy

sections.title: The Three Hidden Sales Inside the Tour
sections.html:
The Three Hidden Sales Inside the Tour

HIDDEN SALE 1

The Inspection Authorization

By the end of the tour, the customer has already mentally agreed to a DVI — we didn’t ask, we just described what we do. Asking after the tour is a formality.

HIDDEN SALE 2

The Price Defense

A customer who sees the shop, the techs, the equipment, and the warranty does not later compare us to Cheap Bob’s on Main. They’ve already mentally bought a different product.

HIDDEN SALE 3

The Repeat Visit

The tour is not just for today — it’s the marketing for the next 5 years of visits. Customers refer the shop they were “walked through,” not the shop they “dropped the car off at.”

sections.title: Scripts
sections.html:
Scripts

SCRIPTThe Shop Tour (walking pace, eye contact, warmth)

“Welcome to [Shop], your certified dealer alternative. We’re proud to have certified technicians ready to take care of all your vehicle needs — tires, maintenance, major repairs. We also partner with trusted local specialists for accessory work like dent repair, body work, and windshield replacement so you’re covered in one place.

To make your visit convenient, we offer complimentary shuttle rides, Uber or Lyft, rental car reimbursement, and loaner vehicles when available. If you stay, the waiting area has complimentary water, coffee, and tea — but there’s no need to wait if transportation works better.

Once you drop off, we keep you informed every step of the way: calls, texts, digital vehicle inspections, and electronic approvals for estimates, invoices, and payment. Clear, simple, transparent.

We’re excited to serve you today, and thank you for trusting [Shop]. Can I get you something to drink — coffee, water, or tea?”

PSYCHOLOGY DECODERWhy we end the tour with the drink offer.

The drink offer is the final reciprocity trigger. It also creates a tiny social contract: the customer is now “a guest” in our space, not “a transaction.” Guests don’t haggle. Guests don’t walk out. Guests authorize inspections.

sections.title: Mastery Check — Gate to Level 2.5
sections.html:
Mastery Check — Gate to Level 2.5

LEVEL 2 · CREDIBILITY MASTERY

  • Delivered full shop tour in under 90 seconds, walking pace, with eye contact.
  • Mentioned all three credibility anchors: certified techs, warranty, transportation options.
  • Offered drink at the end of the tour.
  • Did not brag — demonstrated the operation rather than claiming about it.
  • Customer’s body language visibly relaxed by the end (shoulders down, posture open).

Pass all 5 across three live tours → Credibility certified.

COACH’S CORNERScore the tour with the trust triangle in hand.

Walk along on one tour per advisor per week. For each sentence they deliver, silently tag it C / T / Tw in your notebook. After the tour, show them the tally. Most under-performing advisors over-index on Competence (bragging) and skip Transparency entirely. Once they see the imbalance, it self-corrects within two weeks.

Tour Delivery %
100%
Every first-time customer gets the full tour — no exceptions.
Tour → DVI Consent
≥ 95%
% of toured customers who authorize the digital vehicle inspection.
Tour Duration
75–90 sec
Faster = rushed. Slower = lost.
